info.title: Olympus FOIA Ledger
info.description: Unified Olympus API — append-only cryptographic ledger for NC Public Records (G.S. § 132) and Federal FOIA (5 U.S.C. § 552) requests, plus protocol-layer audit and verification.

post.description: Commit a client-asserted BLAKE3 hash. This endpoint does not canonicalize or hash source documents server-side; it records the caller-supplied fingerprint.

404.description: The request_id does not match any existing public records request.

409.description: The doc_hash has already been committed to the ledger.

post.description:
Verify a previously committed document hash.

Looks up the commit by ``commit_id`` or ``doc_hash`` (at least one is
required), regenerates the Merkle inclusion proof and ZK proof stub,
and returns a verification result.

Args:
    body: Verify request with at least one of commit_id or doc_hash.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Verification result with proof details.

get.description:
Return the global ledger state.

Aggregates the state roots of all shards (currently a single shard) and
returns a summary including total commit count and the most recent epoch.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Global state root, shard count, total commits, and last epoch.

Return the state of a single shard.

Args:
    shard_id: Hex shard identifier.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Shard state root, commit count, and latest commits.

parameters.name: shard_id

schema.description: Shard identifier (alphanumeric, hyphens, colons, dots; max 128 chars)
schema.title: Shard Id

parameters.description: Shard identifier (alphanumeric, hyphens, colons, dots; max 128 chars)

get.description:
Return the Merkle inclusion proof and ZK proof stub for a commit.

Args:
    commit_id: Hex commit identifier (e.g. ``"0xc7d4a2f8e1b3095d"``).
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Merkle proof, ZK proof stub, shard, and epoch.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the commit is not found.

schema.title: Response Get Commit Proof Ledger Proof  Commit Id  Get

get.description:
Return a human-readable feed of recent ledger events.

Returns plain-English descriptions of what has been happening in the
ledger — document submissions, verifications, and errors — suitable
for display to non-technical users.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.
    limit: Maximum number of items to return (1–200, default 50).
    activity_type: Optional filter by activity type
        (e.g. ``"DOCUMENT_SUBMITTED"``, ``"VERIFICATION_SUCCESS"``).

Returns:
    :class:`ActivityFeedResponse` with items ordered newest first.

post.description:
Upload a document to the ledger with guided step-by-step feedback.

Accepts any common document format (PDF, Word, plain text, images) and
returns a plain-English summary of each processing step so non-technical
users understand exactly what happened.

Args:
    request: Raw HTTP request (used to pre-check Content-Length).
    db: Injected async database session.
    file: The document to submit.
    request_id: Optional FOIA request ID to associate with this document.
    description: Optional short description of the document.

Returns:
    :class:`SimpleIngestionResponse` with numbered steps and outcome.

Verify whether a document is recorded in the ledger.

Accepts three verification methods:

1. **Upload file** — computes the fingerprint and checks the ledger.
2. **Record ID** — looks up by permanent record ID (``OLY-NNNN``) or raw commit ID.
3. **Document hash** — looks up directly by BLAKE3 hex hash.

Returns a plain-English verdict with step-by-step proof details.

Args:
    request: Raw HTTP request (used to pre-check Content-Length).
    db: Injected async database session.
    file: Optional uploaded file to verify by content.
    commit_id: Optional record ID or raw commit ID.
    doc_hash: Optional pre-computed BLAKE3 hex hash.

Returns:
    :class:`SimpleVerificationResponse` with a clear verified/not-found verdict.

Raises:
    HTTPException 400: If none of the three inputs are provided.

File a new public-records or FOIA request.

Computes a BLAKE3 commit hash over the canonical request content,
assigns the request to the default ledger shard, and calculates the
statutory deadline.

Args:
    body: Request payload.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Created request with commit_hash, display_id, and deadline.

get.description:
Return a paginated list of public-records requests.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.
    status: Optional status filter.
    agency_id: Optional agency UUID filter.
    search: Optional full-text search on subject.
    page: Page number (1-indexed).
    per_page: Page size.

Returns:
    List of matching request summaries.

Return full details of a public-records request.

Args:
    display_id: Human-readable identifier, e.g. ``"OLY-0042"``.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Full request detail.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the request is not found.

patch.description:
Update the status of a request and anchor the change to the ledger.

Enforces a state-machine on allowed transitions to prevent illegal
lifecycle changes (e.g. FULFILLED → PENDING).

Args:
    display_id: Human-readable identifier of the request.
    body: New status and optional note.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Updated request.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the request is not found.
    HTTPException 409: If the transition is not allowed.

post.description:
Create a new agency record.

Args:
    body: Agency creation payload.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Created agency.

get.description:
Return a list of agencies with computed statistics.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.
    level: Optional jurisdictional level filter.
    search: Optional name search.

Returns:
    List of agencies.

Return full details of an agency.

Args:
    agency_id: UUID of the agency.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Agency detail.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the agency is not found.

Return all appeals.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    List of all appeal records.

post.description:
File an appeal against an agency response.

Rejects the appeal if the underlying request has already been fulfilled.
Sets the request status to APPEALED on success.

Args:
    body: Appeal payload.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Created appeal record.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the request is not found.
    HTTPException 409: If the request has already been fulfilled.

get.description:
Return a single appeal by ID.

Args:
    appeal_id: UUID of the appeal.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Appeal detail.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the appeal is not found.

post.description:
Issue a new SBT-style non-transferable credential.

Anchors the credential issuance to the ledger via a generated commit_id.

Args:
    body: Credential creation payload.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Created credential record.

delete.description:
Revoke a credential by setting its revoked_at timestamp.

The credential record is retained for audit purposes; Olympus is an
append-only ledger and does not hard-delete records.

Only the API key that originally issued a credential may revoke it.
Credentials without an ``issued_by_key_id`` (created before this check
was introduced) can be revoked by any write-key holder.

Args:
    credential_id: UUID of the credential to revoke.
    db: Injected async database session.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the credential is not found or owned by a different key.
    HTTPException 409: If the credential has already been revoked.

post.description:
Generate a new API key and return the raw key + env-var JSON entry.

Protected by ``X-Admin-Key``. The raw key is returned once — the caller
must store it. The ``env_entry`` field is the JSON blob to add to
``OLYMPUS_API_KEYS_JSON`` in your .env file, then call reload-keys.

Force a hot reload of FOIA API keys from the environment.

Protected by a separate ``OLYMPUS_ADMIN_KEY`` secret.  This endpoint
allows key rotation and revocation without restarting the API process.

Raises:
    HTTPException 401: If the provided ``X-Admin-Key`` header is missing or wrong.
    HTTPException 503: If ``OLYMPUS_ADMIN_KEY`` is not configured on the server.

schema.title: Response Admin Reload Keys Key Admin Reload Keys Post

    version: Record version (must be >= 1)

Returns:

Get the last N ledger entries for a shard.

Entries are returned in reverse chronological order (most recent first).
Use this to verify the ledger chain linkage.

Args:

Returns:

get.description:
Verify the Ed25519 signature and RFC 3161 timestamp of the latest shard header.

Optionally performs paginated replay verification of the shard's Merkle
tree history using the ``max_headers`` / ``after_seq`` cursor parameters
(RFC 6962 §4.6 cursor model).

Returns both the signature validity and, if a timestamp token has been stored,
the RFC 3161 token and whether it is cryptographically valid.

Args:
    shard_id: Shard identifier.
    max_headers: Max headers to verify per call (cursor pagination).
    after_seq: Resume verification after this sequence number.

Returns:
    Verification result including signature status, timestamp token,
    timestamp validity, and optional replay cursor.

parameters.name: shard_id

schema.description: Shard identifier (alphanumeric, hyphens, colons, dots; max 128 chars)
schema.title: Shard Id

parameters.description: Shard identifier (alphanumeric, hyphens, colons, dots; max 128 chars)

parameters.name: max_headers

schema.description: Max headers to verify in this call. Use next_seq cursor for subsequent pages.
schema.title: Max Headers

parameters.description: Max headers to verify in this call. Use next_seq cursor for subsequent pages.

parameters.name: after_seq

schema.description: Resume verification after this sequence number.
schema.title: After Seq

parameters.description: Resume verification after this sequence number.

get.description:
Prometheus metrics endpoint.

Returns all registered Olympus metrics in Prometheus text exposition
format (``Content-Type: text/plain; version=0.0.4``).

Metrics exposed:
- ``olympus_proof_generation_seconds`` — histogram of proof latency by operation.
- ``olympus_ledger_height`` — current ledger height.
- ``olympus_smt_root_divergence_total`` — counter of SMT root divergence events.
- ``olympus_ingest_operations_total`` — counter of ingest outcomes.

Returns HTTP 503 if the ``prometheus-client`` library is not installed.

post.description:
Record an SMT root divergence event between this node and a remote peer.

This endpoint is called by inter-node health-check processes when they
detect that the SMT root for a shard differs between nodes.  It increments
the ``olympus_smt_root_divergence_total`` Prometheus counter and emits a
structured warning log so that alerting rules can fire.

Args:
    shard_id:    The shard whose SMT root diverged.
    local_root:  Hex-encoded SMT root computed on this node.
    remote_root: Hex-encoded SMT root reported by the remote peer.
    remote_node: Identifier of the remote peer.

Returns:
    Confirmation that the divergence event was recorded.

parameters.name: shard_id

schema.description: Shard identifier (alphanumeric, hyphens, colons, dots; max 128 chars)
schema.title: Shard Id

parameters.description: Shard identifier (alphanumeric, hyphens, colons, dots; max 128 chars)

parameters.name: local_root

schema.description: Hex-encoded local SMT root
schema.title: Local Root

parameters.description: Hex-encoded local SMT root

parameters.name: remote_root

schema.description: Hex-encoded remote SMT root
schema.title: Remote Root

parameters.description: Hex-encoded remote SMT root

parameters.name: remote_node

schema.description: Remote node identifier (URL or node ID)
schema.title: Remote Node

parameters.description: Remote node identifier (URL or node ID)

schema.title: Response Alert Smt Divergence Shards  Shard Id  Alert Smt Divergence Post

get.description:
Get the most recent Rekor transparency log anchor for a shard.

This endpoint returns the latest Sigstore Rekor anchor for the specified
shard, including the verification URL for independent verification.

Args:
    shard_id: Shard identifier.

Returns:
    The latest Rekor anchor for the shard, or 404 if none exists.

parameters.name: shard_id

schema.description: Shard identifier (alphanumeric, hyphens, colons, dots; max 128 chars)
schema.title: Shard Id

parameters.description: Shard identifier (alphanumeric, hyphens, colons, dots; max 128 chars)

post.description:
Atomically ingest a batch of records.

Each record is canonicalized, hashed, and checked for duplicates.
Non-duplicate records are committed to a Merkle tree and appended
to the ledger.  The response includes proof IDs for async proof
retrieval.

Supports multi-shard batches by grouping records by shard_id and
processing each group separately. Canonicalization is parallelized
across the batch to maximize throughput on training dataset scale.

If PostgreSQL is configured (DATABASE_URL and OLYMPUS_INGEST_SIGNING_KEY),
records are persisted durably. Otherwise, they are stored in-memory only.

Args:
    batch: Batch of records to ingest.

Returns:
    Ingestion results with proof IDs.

Ingest a file by its raw bytes — no JSON wrapper, no canonicalization.

The committed `content_hash` is plain BLAKE3 of the file bytes (the same
value `blake3sum file` produces and the same value the in-browser hasher
computes), so dropping the same file again will round-trip verify.

Distinct from POST /ingest/records, which canonicalizes a JSON document
and hashes the canonical form.

schema.title: Response Ingest Raw File Ingest Files Post

get.description:
Retrieve the proof for a previously ingested record.

Args:
    proof_id: Proof identifier returned by the ingestion endpoint.

Returns:
    Full ingestion proof including Merkle proof and ledger entry hash.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if proof_id is not found.

Verify that a committed BLAKE3 content hash exists in the ingestion store.

This endpoint returns the stored proof bundle plus a server-side Merkle proof
verification result so public portals can display both the commitment data and
the verifiable transcript needed for independent re-checking.

Requires the ``verify`` scope. See ``docs/SECURITY_AUDIT_REPORT.md`` (L-1) for
the audit finding that established authentication on this route.

post.description: Verify an externally supplied proof bundle without persisting it.

Retrieve the server-computed proof bundle for a committed document.

The caller supplies only the raw document bytes. The server
canonicalizes, hashes, and looks up its authoritative proof record.
No caller-supplied metadata is accepted — all proof fields are
derived from the server's own ingestion records.

Returns 404 if the document has not been committed yet. Commit
first via POST /ingest/records.

Commit a pre-computed artifact hash to the Olympus ledger.

This endpoint is the primary integration point for CI/CD pipelines.
The caller is responsible for computing the BLAKE3 hash of the artifact
before calling this endpoint.  The hash is committed to an append-only
ledger entry, and a proof ID is returned for future verification.

If PostgreSQL is configured, the artifact is persisted durably.

Args:
    request: Artifact commit request with hash, namespace, and id.

Returns:
    Commitment response with proof_id and ledger anchor details.

get.description:
Get the latest Signed Tree Head for a shard.

This endpoint returns the most recent STH committed for the specified shard.
Observers can use this to collect STHs from multiple nodes and detect
split-view logs by comparing roots and signatures.

Args:
    shard_id: Shard identifier

Returns:
    Latest Signed Tree Head for the shard

Raises:
    404: If the shard does not exist or has no STH
    503: If the database is not available

parameters.name: shard_id

schema.description: Shard identifier
schema.title: Shard Id

parameters.description: Shard identifier

get.description:
Get recent STH history for a shard.

Returns the most recent N Signed Tree Heads for the specified shard.
Observers can use this to:
- Verify append-only growth by checking consistency proofs
- Detect rollbacks or forks
- Compare historical STHs across nodes

Args:
    shard_id: Shard identifier
    n: Number of STHs to retrieve (1-100, default 10)

Returns:
    List of recent STHs in reverse chronological order (most recent first)

Raises:
    404: If the shard does not exist
    503: If the database is not available

parameters.name: shard_id

schema.description: Shard identifier
schema.title: Shard Id

parameters.description: Shard identifier

parameters.name: n

schema.description: Number of STHs to retrieve
schema.title: N

parameters.description: Number of STHs to retrieve

Return the announcement with the highest checkpoint sequence.

Raises:
    404: If no announcements have been stored yet.

Return any stored announcement whose checkpoint.sequence matches.

Args:
    sequence: Checkpoint sequence number to look up.

Raises:
    404: If no announcement for that sequence exists.

Return a paginated list of announcements sorted by sequence descending.

Args:
    limit: Maximum number of results (default 20).
    offset: Number of results to skip for pagination.

post.description:
Submit a checkpoint announcement from an origin node.

Args:
    request: Announcement containing the origin identifier, checkpoint,
        and a unique nonce.
    _api_key: Injected API-key dependency — callers must provide a valid
        key via ``X-API-Key`` header or ``Authorization: Bearer`` token.

Returns:
    201 confirmation with origin, sequence, and status.

Raises:
    409: If an announcement from the same origin at the same sequence
         already exists, or if the nonce has been seen before.
    422: If the checkpoint timestamp is stale (older than
         ``_MAX_ANNOUNCE_SKEW_SECONDS``).

get.description:
Return split-view evidence detected across stored announcements.

Groups announcements by checkpoint.sequence.  Any sequence where two or
more distinct origins reported differing checkpoint_hash values is
considered a conflict.

Returns:
    List of conflict entries (empty if no conflicts exist).

schema.title: Response Get Gossip State Witness Gossip Get

get.description: Return service health and current observation count.
Return the latest signed-root envelope for monitor polling.

schema.title: Response Get Latest Signed Root Transparency V1 Signed Root Get

get.description: Return a signed-root envelope at a specific tree height.

schema.title: Response Get Signed Root By Height Transparency V1 Signed Root  Height  Get

get.description: Return registered witness public keys for independent verification.

schema.title: Response Get Witnesses Transparency V1 Witnesses Get

get.description: Return an inclusion proof anchored to the latest signed root.

schema.title: Response Get Inclusion Transparency V1 Inclusion  Key  Get

get.description: Return a non-inclusion proof anchored to the latest signed root.

schema.title: Response Get Non Inclusion Transparency V1 Non Inclusion  Key  Get

post.description: Accept split-view evidence for public monitor verification workflows.

schema.title: Response Submit Equivocation Evidence Transparency V1 Gossip Equivocation Post

post.description:
Anchor a dataset manifest to the Olympus ledger.

Validates the caller's Ed25519 signature, computes deterministic hashes
(content-only, no timestamp in identity), checks for replay via
uniqueness constraint, requests an RFC 3161 timestamp, and persists
the commitment record.

get.description: List datasets with optional filters and pagination.
Return the latest commit for a logical dataset.

Run independent verification checks on a committed dataset.

Checks:
  1. commit_id matches deterministic recomputation (no timestamp)
  2. Ed25519 signature is valid
  3. RFC 3161 timestamp status is "verified"
  4. Commit chain integrity
  5. Key revocation cross-reference
  6. Merkle inclusion proof

Generate a self-contained proof bundle for a dataset commit.

The proof bundle includes the commit metadata, file list, Merkle
inclusion proof, and cryptographic verification results so that an
external auditor can independently verify the dataset commitment.

As a side-effect, the ``proof_bundle_uri`` field on the underlying
artifact is populated with the full URL so that subsequent
``GET /datasets/{dataset_id}`` responses include it.
Return ordered version history for a logical dataset.

parameters.name: n

schema.description: Maximum number of versions to return (1-1000).
schema.title: N

parameters.description: Maximum number of versions to return (1-1000).

post.description: Record that a model consumed this dataset.

Sign a shard header on behalf of this Guardian node.

This endpoint:
1. Verifies the request domain matches the federation vote domain
2. Checks if the shard_root matches the local node's current committed root
3. If roots match, signs and returns the signature
4. If roots differ, returns a 409 Conflict with fork evidence

Args:
    request: The FederationVoteMessage and header to sign.

Returns:
    NodeSignature with this node's signature.

Raises:
    HTTPException 503: If Guardian replication is not enabled.
    HTTPException 409: If a fork is detected (root mismatch).
    HTTPException 500: If signing fails.

get.description:
Get the status of Guardian replication on this node.

Returns:
    Status information including whether Guardian mode is enabled,
    the local node ID, and registry information.

schema.title: Response Federation Status Federation Status Get

get.description:
Return aggregated platform metrics.

MRR is computed dynamically as the average purchase price multiplied by the
number of paid subscribers.  When no purchases exist yet the average
defaults to zero, yielding an MRR of zero.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    MRR, total revenue, user count, and conversion rate.

Return a paginated list of customers ordered by creation date (newest first).

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.
    page: Page number (1-indexed).
    per_page: Page size (1–100, default 20).

Returns:
    Paginated customer list with total count.

Export customers as a CSV file with a bounded row limit.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.
    max_rows: Maximum number of rows to export (1–50 000, default 50 000).

Returns:
    Streaming CSV response with ``Content-Disposition: attachment``.

Return aggregated public ledger statistics from an async GET endpoint.

Args:
    None.

Returns:
    PublicStats: Counts for copies, shards, proofs, and process uptime.

post.description: Create a new user account and issue a first API key.

Create a user with admin-selected scopes.

This is the local/operator path for onboarding users. It is protected by
``X-Admin-Key`` or an admin-scoped ``X-API-Key`` and defaults new users to
verify-only access (``read`` + ``verify``). The raw API key is returned once.
Verify password and return the user's active API keys.

Issue a fresh API key using email + password — no existing key required.

Recovery path for users whose stored key is lost, expired, or invalid.
Scopes are capped to the non-admin self-service set regardless of what
is requested; admin scope requires out-of-band provisioning.

get.description: List active API keys for the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List Keys Auth Keys Get

post.description:
Generate an additional API key for the authenticated user.

The new key's scopes must be a subset of the caller's current key scopes
(and members of the global allowlist) to prevent privilege escalation via
self-minted keys.

delete.description: Revoke one of the authenticated user's API keys.

Delete the caller's account and all associated API keys.

Accepts email + password — no existing API key required, so users who have
lost or invalidated their key can still close their account and re-register.

Admin: delete any user account and all their API keys.

Protected by X-Admin-Key header.

parameters.description: Shard identifier (alphanumeric, hyphens, colons, dots; max 128 chars)

get.description:
Return the Merkle inclusion proof and ZK proof stub for a commit.

Args:
    commit_id: Hex commit identifier (e.g. ``"0xc7d4a2f8e1b3095d"``).
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Merkle proof, ZK proof stub, shard, and epoch.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the commit is not found.

schema.title: Response Get Commit Proof V1 Ledger Proof  Commit Id  Get

get.description:
Return a human-readable feed of recent ledger events.

Returns plain-English descriptions of what has been happening in the
ledger — document submissions, verifications, and errors — suitable
for display to non-technical users.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.
    limit: Maximum number of items to return (1–200, default 50).
    activity_type: Optional filter by activity type
        (e.g. ``"DOCUMENT_SUBMITTED"``, ``"VERIFICATION_SUCCESS"``).

Returns:
    :class:`ActivityFeedResponse` with items ordered newest first.

post.description:
Upload a document to the ledger with guided step-by-step feedback.

Accepts any common document format (PDF, Word, plain text, images) and
returns a plain-English summary of each processing step so non-technical
users understand exactly what happened.

Args:
    request: Raw HTTP request (used to pre-check Content-Length).
    db: Injected async database session.
    file: The document to submit.
    request_id: Optional FOIA request ID to associate with this document.
    description: Optional short description of the document.

Returns:
    :class:`SimpleIngestionResponse` with numbered steps and outcome.

Verify whether a document is recorded in the ledger.

Accepts three verification methods:

1. **Upload file** — computes the fingerprint and checks the ledger.
2. **Record ID** — looks up by permanent record ID (``OLY-NNNN``) or raw commit ID.
3. **Document hash** — looks up directly by BLAKE3 hex hash.

Returns a plain-English verdict with step-by-step proof details.

Args:
    request: Raw HTTP request (used to pre-check Content-Length).
    db: Injected async database session.
    file: Optional uploaded file to verify by content.
    commit_id: Optional record ID or raw commit ID.
    doc_hash: Optional pre-computed BLAKE3 hex hash.

Returns:
    :class:`SimpleVerificationResponse` with a clear verified/not-found verdict.

Raises:
    HTTPException 400: If none of the three inputs are provided.

File a new public-records or FOIA request.

Computes a BLAKE3 commit hash over the canonical request content,
assigns the request to the default ledger shard, and calculates the
statutory deadline.

Args:
    body: Request payload.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Created request with commit_hash, display_id, and deadline.

get.description:
Return a paginated list of public-records requests.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.
    status: Optional status filter.
    agency_id: Optional agency UUID filter.
    search: Optional full-text search on subject.
    page: Page number (1-indexed).
    per_page: Page size.

Returns:
    List of matching request summaries.

schema.title: Response List Requests V1 Requests Get

get.description:
Return full details of a public-records request.

Args:
    display_id: Human-readable identifier, e.g. ``"OLY-0042"``.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Full request detail.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the request is not found.

patch.description:
Update the status of a request and anchor the change to the ledger.

Enforces a state-machine on allowed transitions to prevent illegal
lifecycle changes (e.g. FULFILLED → PENDING).

Args:
    display_id: Human-readable identifier of the request.
    body: New status and optional note.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Updated request.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the request is not found.
    HTTPException 409: If the transition is not allowed.

post.description:
Create a new agency record.

Args:
    body: Agency creation payload.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Created agency.

get.description:
Return a list of agencies with computed statistics.

Args:

    search: Optional name search.

Returns:
    List of agencies.

schema.title: Response List Agencies V1 Agencies Get

Return full details of an agency.

Raises:

Return all appeals.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    List of all appeal records.

schema.title: Response List Appeals V1 Appeals Get

post.description:
File an appeal against an agency response.

Rejects the appeal if the underlying request has already been fulfilled.
Sets the request status to APPEALED on success.

Args:
    body: Appeal payload.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Created appeal record.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the request is not found.
    HTTPException 409: If the request has already been fulfilled.

get.description:
Return a single appeal by ID.

Args:
    appeal_id: UUID of the appeal.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Appeal detail.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the appeal is not found.

post.description:
Issue a new SBT-style non-transferable credential.

Anchors the credential issuance to the ledger via a generated commit_id.

Args:
    body: Credential creation payload.
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    Created credential record.

delete.description:
Revoke a credential by setting its revoked_at timestamp.

The credential record is retained for audit purposes; Olympus is an
append-only ledger and does not hard-delete records.

Only the API key that originally issued a credential may revoke it.
Credentials without an ``issued_by_key_id`` (created before this check
was introduced) can be revoked by any write-key holder.

Args:
    credential_id: UUID of the credential to revoke.
    db: Injected async database session.

Raises:
    HTTPException 404: If the credential is not found or owned by a different key.
    HTTPException 409: If the credential has already been revoked.

post.description:
Generate a new API key and return the raw key + env-var JSON entry.

Protected by ``X-Admin-Key``. The raw key is returned once — the caller
must store it. The ``env_entry`` field is the JSON blob to add to
``OLYMPUS_API_KEYS_JSON`` in your .env file, then call reload-keys.

Force a hot reload of FOIA API keys from the environment.

Protected by a separate ``OLYMPUS_ADMIN_KEY`` secret.  This endpoint
allows key rotation and revocation without restarting the API process.

Raises:
    HTTPException 401: If the provided ``X-Admin-Key`` header is missing or wrong.
    HTTPException 503: If ``OLYMPUS_ADMIN_KEY`` is not configured on the server.

schema.title: Response Admin Reload Keys V1 Key Admin Reload Keys Post

get.description:
Verify the Ed25519 signature and RFC 3161 timestamp of the latest shard header.

Optionally performs paginated replay verification of the shard's Merkle
tree history using the ``max_headers`` / ``after_seq`` cursor parameters
(RFC 6962 §4.6 cursor model).

Returns both the signature validity and, if a timestamp token has been stored,
the RFC 3161 token and whether it is cryptographically valid.

Args:
    shard_id: Shard identifier.
    max_headers: Max headers to verify per call (cursor pagination).
    after_seq: Resume verification after this sequence number.

Returns:
    Verification result including signature status, timestamp token,
    timestamp validity, and optional replay cursor.

get.description:
Prometheus metrics endpoint.

Returns all registered Olympus metrics in Prometheus text exposition
format (``Content-Type: text/plain; version=0.0.4``).

Metrics exposed:
- ``olympus_proof_generation_seconds`` — histogram of proof latency by operation.
- ``olympus_ledger_height`` — current ledger height.
- ``olympus_smt_root_divergence_total`` — counter of SMT root divergence events.
- ``olympus_ingest_operations_total`` — counter of ingest outcomes.

Returns HTTP 503 if the ``prometheus-client`` library is not installed.

post.description:
Record an SMT root divergence event between this node and a remote peer.

This endpoint is called by inter-node health-check processes when they
detect that the SMT root for a shard differs between nodes.  It increments
the ``olympus_smt_root_divergence_total`` Prometheus counter and emits a
structured warning log so that alerting rules can fire.

Args:
    shard_id:    The shard whose SMT root diverged.
    local_root:  Hex-encoded SMT root computed on this node.
    remote_root: Hex-encoded SMT root reported by the remote peer.
    remote_node: Identifier of the remote peer.

Returns:
    Confirmation that the divergence event was recorded.

parameters.name: shard_id

parameters.name: remote_root

Returns:

post.description:
Atomically ingest a batch of records.

Each record is canonicalized, hashed, and checked for duplicates.
Non-duplicate records are committed to a Merkle tree and appended
to the ledger.  The response includes proof IDs for async proof
retrieval.

Supports multi-shard batches by grouping records by shard_id and
processing each group separately. Canonicalization is parallelized
across the batch to maximize throughput on training dataset scale.

If PostgreSQL is configured (DATABASE_URL and OLYMPUS_INGEST_SIGNING_KEY),
records are persisted durably. Otherwise, they are stored in-memory only.

Args:
    batch: Batch of records to ingest.

Returns:
    Ingestion results with proof IDs.

Ingest a file by its raw bytes — no JSON wrapper, no canonicalization.

The committed `content_hash` is plain BLAKE3 of the file bytes (the same
value `blake3sum file` produces and the same value the in-browser hasher
computes), so dropping the same file again will round-trip verify.

Distinct from POST /ingest/records, which canonicalizes a JSON document
and hashes the canonical form.

schema.title: Response Ingest Raw File V1 Ingest Files Post

get.description:
Retrieve the proof for a previously ingested record.

Args:
    proof_id: Proof identifier returned by the ingestion endpoint.

Returns:
    Full ingestion proof including Merkle proof and ledger entry hash.

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if proof_id is not found.

Verify that a committed BLAKE3 content hash exists in the ingestion store.

This endpoint returns the stored proof bundle plus a server-side Merkle proof
verification result so public portals can display both the commitment data and
the verifiable transcript needed for independent re-checking.

Requires the ``verify`` scope. See ``docs/SECURITY_AUDIT_REPORT.md`` (L-1) for
the audit finding that established authentication on this route.

post.description: Verify an externally supplied proof bundle without persisting it.

Retrieve the server-computed proof bundle for a committed document.

The caller supplies only the raw document bytes. The server
canonicalizes, hashes, and looks up its authoritative proof record.
No caller-supplied metadata is accepted — all proof fields are
derived from the server's own ingestion records.

Returns 404 if the document has not been committed yet. Commit
first via POST /ingest/records.

Commit a pre-computed artifact hash to the Olympus ledger.

This endpoint is the primary integration point for CI/CD pipelines.
The caller is responsible for computing the BLAKE3 hash of the artifact
before calling this endpoint.  The hash is committed to an append-only
ledger entry, and a proof ID is returned for future verification.

If PostgreSQL is configured, the artifact is persisted durably.

Args:
    request: Artifact commit request with hash, namespace, and id.

Returns:
    Commitment response with proof_id and ledger anchor details.

get.description:
Get the latest Signed Tree Head for a shard.

This endpoint returns the most recent STH committed for the specified shard.
Observers can use this to collect STHs from multiple nodes and detect
split-view logs by comparing roots and signatures.

Args:
    shard_id: Shard identifier

Returns:
    Latest Signed Tree Head for the shard

Raises:
    404: If the shard does not exist or has no STH
    503: If the database is not available

parameters.name: shard_id

schema.description: Shard identifier
schema.title: Shard Id

parameters.description: Shard identifier

get.description:
Get recent STH history for a shard.

Returns the most recent N Signed Tree Heads for the specified shard.
Observers can use this to:
- Verify append-only growth by checking consistency proofs
- Detect rollbacks or forks
- Compare historical STHs across nodes

Args:
    shard_id: Shard identifier
    n: Number of STHs to retrieve (1-100, default 10)

Returns:
    List of recent STHs in reverse chronological order (most recent first)

Raises:
    404: If the shard does not exist
    503: If the database is not available

parameters.name: shard_id

schema.description: Shard identifier
schema.title: Shard Id

parameters.description: Shard identifier

parameters.name: n

schema.description: Number of STHs to retrieve
schema.title: N

parameters.description: Number of STHs to retrieve

Return the announcement with the highest checkpoint sequence.

Raises:

Raises:

Return a paginated list of announcements sorted by sequence descending.

Args:

    offset: Number of results to skip for pagination.

post.description:
Submit a checkpoint announcement from an origin node.

Args:
    request: Announcement containing the origin identifier, checkpoint,
        and a unique nonce.
    _api_key: Injected API-key dependency — callers must provide a valid
        key via ``X-API-Key`` header or ``Authorization: Bearer`` token.

Returns:
    201 confirmation with origin, sequence, and status.

Raises:
    409: If an announcement from the same origin at the same sequence
         already exists, or if the nonce has been seen before.
    422: If the checkpoint timestamp is stale (older than
         ``_MAX_ANNOUNCE_SKEW_SECONDS``).

get.description:
Return split-view evidence detected across stored announcements.

Groups announcements by checkpoint.sequence.  Any sequence where two or
more distinct origins reported differing checkpoint_hash values is
considered a conflict.

Returns:
    List of conflict entries (empty if no conflicts exist).

schema.title: Response Get Gossip State V1 Witness Gossip Get

get.description: Return service health and current observation count.

post.description:
Anchor a dataset manifest to the Olympus ledger.

Validates the caller's Ed25519 signature, computes deterministic hashes
(content-only, no timestamp in identity), checks for replay via
uniqueness constraint, requests an RFC 3161 timestamp, and persists
the commitment record.

get.description: List datasets with optional filters and pagination.
Return the latest commit for a logical dataset.

Run independent verification checks on a committed dataset.

Checks:
  1. commit_id matches deterministic recomputation (no timestamp)
  2. Ed25519 signature is valid
  3. RFC 3161 timestamp status is "verified"
  4. Commit chain integrity
  5. Key revocation cross-reference
  6. Merkle inclusion proof

Generate a self-contained proof bundle for a dataset commit.

The proof bundle includes the commit metadata, file list, Merkle
inclusion proof, and cryptographic verification results so that an
external auditor can independently verify the dataset commitment.

As a side-effect, the ``proof_bundle_uri`` field on the underlying
artifact is populated with the full URL so that subsequent
``GET /datasets/{dataset_id}`` responses include it.
Return ordered version history for a logical dataset.

parameters.name: n

schema.description: Maximum number of versions to return (1-1000).
schema.title: N

parameters.description: Maximum number of versions to return (1-1000).

post.description: Record that a model consumed this dataset.

Sign a shard header on behalf of this Guardian node.

This endpoint:
1. Verifies the request domain matches the federation vote domain
2. Checks if the shard_root matches the local node's current committed root
3. If roots match, signs and returns the signature
4. If roots differ, returns a 409 Conflict with fork evidence

Args:
    request: The FederationVoteMessage and header to sign.

Returns:
    NodeSignature with this node's signature.

Raises:
    HTTPException 503: If Guardian replication is not enabled.
    HTTPException 409: If a fork is detected (root mismatch).
    HTTPException 500: If signing fails.

get.description:
Get the status of Guardian replication on this node.

Returns:
    Status information including whether Guardian mode is enabled,
    the local node ID, and registry information.

schema.title: Response Federation Status V1 Federation Status Get

get.description:
Return aggregated platform metrics.

MRR is computed dynamically as the average purchase price multiplied by the
number of paid subscribers.  When no purchases exist yet the average
defaults to zero, yielding an MRR of zero.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.

Returns:
    MRR, total revenue, user count, and conversion rate.

Return a paginated list of customers ordered by creation date (newest first).

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.
    page: Page number (1-indexed).
    per_page: Page size (1–100, default 20).

Returns:
    Paginated customer list with total count.

Export customers as a CSV file with a bounded row limit.

Args:
    db: Injected async database session.
    max_rows: Maximum number of rows to export (1–50 000, default 50 000).

Returns:
    Streaming CSV response with ``Content-Disposition: attachment``.

Return aggregated public ledger statistics from an async GET endpoint.

Args:
    None.

Returns:
    PublicStats: Counts for copies, shards, proofs, and process uptime.
Serve the bundled UI when present; otherwise expose API version info.

Health check with database and sequencer status.

Returns:
    JSON response with service health indicators:
    - status: "ok" | "degraded" (overall health)
    - version: API version string
    - database: "connected" | "degraded" | "error" | "not_initialized"
    - db_check: True if database SELECT 1 succeeds
    - sequencer: "ok" | "degraded" | "unavailable" | "disabled"
      (only present when storage_layer is importable)

A single entry in the activity feed.

    timestamp: When the activity occurred.
    activity_type: Machine-readable category.

    description: Plain-English explanation.
    related_commit_id: Technical commit reference (optional).
    related_request_id: FOIA request reference (optional).
    user_friendly_status: Display badge.

ArtifactCommitRequest.title: ArtifactCommitRequest

Request body for committing a pre-computed artifact hash to the ledger.

Security boundary:
    ``id`` and ``namespace`` are validated by ``IDENTIFIER_PATTERN`` at API
    ingestion time before persistence. This keeps externally supplied
    artifact identifiers constrained before they can flow into downstream
    proof tooling and subprocess-based proof backends.

DocVerifyRequest.description:
Request body for POST /doc/verify.

At least one of ``commit_id`` or ``doc_hash`` must be provided.

ExistenceProofResponse.title: ExistenceProofResponse
ExistenceProofResponse.description: Existence proof with all data for offline verification.

sequence.title: Sequence
sequence.description: Sequence number where the conflict was detected

conflicting_origins.title: Conflicting Origins
conflicting_origins.description: Origins that reported differing hashes at this sequence

hashes.title: Hashes
hashes.description: Mapping of origin identifier to checkpoint hash

GossipConflictEntry.title: GossipConflictEntry
GossipConflictEntry.description: A single split-view conflict detected by gossip analysis.

poseidon_root.title: Poseidon Root
poseidon_root.description: Optional Poseidon root associated with the commitment

hash_suite.title: Hash Suite
hash_suite.description: Poseidon hash suite identifier (e.g. 'poseidon-bn254-v1') — see ADR-0009

HashVerificationResponse.title: HashVerificationResponse
HashVerificationResponse.description: Verification result for a committed content hash.

HeaderVerificationResponse.title: HeaderVerificationResponse
HeaderVerificationResponse.description: Combined Ed25519 signature and RFC 3161 timestamp verification result.

poseidon_root.title: Poseidon Root
poseidon_root.description: Optional Poseidon root associated with the commitment

hash_suite.title: Hash Suite
hash_suite.description: Poseidon hash suite identifier (e.g. 'poseidon-bn254-v1') — see ADR-0009

deduplicated.title: Deduplicated
deduplicated.description: True if record was already present

idempotent.title: Idempotent
idempotent.description: True when this response returns an existing record instead of creating a new one. Callers can use this to distinguish a fresh insert from a deduplicated return.

IngestionResult.title: IngestionResult
IngestionResult.description: Result for a single ingested record.

LedgerTailResponse.title: LedgerTailResponse
LedgerTailResponse.description: Last N ledger entries for a shard.

NonExistenceProofResponse.title: NonExistenceProofResponse
NonExistenceProofResponse.description: Non-existence proof with all data for offline verification.

PendingProofResponse.title: PendingProofResponse
PendingProofResponse.description: Returned when ZK proof generation is pending ceremony completion.

ProcessStep.title: ProcessStep
ProcessStep.description:
A single step in a multi-step operation shown to the user.

Attributes:
    step_number: 1-based position in the process.
    title: Short label for this step.
    status: One of ``pending``, ``in_progress``, ``complete``, ``failed``.
    icon: Display icon string (e.g. ``"✓"``, ``"✗"``, ``"⏳"``).
    message: Human-readable description of what happened.
    details: Optional technical details for advanced users.
    timestamp: When this step completed.

poseidon_root.title: Poseidon Root
poseidon_root.description: Optional Poseidon root associated with the commitment

hash_suite.title: Hash Suite
hash_suite.description: Poseidon hash suite identifier (e.g. 'poseidon-bn254-v1') — see ADR-0009

ProofSubmissionResponse.title: ProofSubmissionResponse
ProofSubmissionResponse.description: Response body for a proof bundle submitted to the ingest API.

content_hash.title: Content Hash
content_hash.description: Hex-encoded BLAKE3 hash committed by Olympus

merkle_root.title: Merkle Root
merkle_root.description: Hex-encoded Merkle root anchoring the content hash

ProofVerificationRequest.title: ProofVerificationRequest
ProofVerificationRequest.description: Request body for server-side verification of a proof bundle.

ProofVerificationResponse.title: ProofVerificationResponse
ProofVerificationResponse.description: Server-side verification result for a submitted proof bundle.

RekorAnchorResponse.title: RekorAnchorResponse
RekorAnchorResponse.description: Rekor transparency log anchor for a shard header.

RequestResponse.title: RequestResponse
RequestResponse.description: Full response representation of a public-records request.

RequestStatus.title: RequestStatus
RequestStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a public-records request.

ShardHeaderResponse.title: ShardHeaderResponse
ShardHeaderResponse.description: Shard header with signature for verification.

ShardHistoryResponse.title: ShardHistoryResponse
ShardHistoryResponse.description: Recent shard history for a shard.

header.title: Header
header.description: Full shard header for fork detection

SignHeaderRequest.title: SignHeaderRequest
SignHeaderRequest.description: Request body for the sign-header endpoint.

SignHeaderResponse.title: SignHeaderResponse
SignHeaderResponse.description: Response body for the sign-header endpoint.

SimpleIngestionResponse.title: SimpleIngestionResponse
SimpleIngestionResponse.description:
User-friendly response for POST /ledger/ingest/simple.

Attributes:
    success: Whether the document was successfully added to the ledger.
    summary: One-sentence plain-English outcome.
    steps: Ordered list of processing steps with status indicators.
    commit_id: Technical commit identifier (for reference).
    permanent_record_id: Display-friendly ID such as ``"OLY-0123"``.
    what_this_means: Plain-English explanation of what was done.
    next_steps: Suggested actions for the user after a successful submission.
    error_help: Guidance on how to resolve the problem (only set on failure).

SimpleVerificationResponse.title: SimpleVerificationResponse
SimpleVerificationResponse.description:
User-friendly response for POST /ledger/verify/simple.

Attributes:
    verified: Whether the document was found in the ledger.
    summary: One-sentence plain-English verdict.
    confidence: How certain the system is (``"certain"`` or ``"uncertain"``).
    recorded_date: When the document was originally recorded.
    related_request: Display ID of the FOIA request this document belongs to.
    proof_details: Step-by-step breakdown of the verification process.
    what_this_means: Plain-English explanation of the verdict.
    why_this_matters: Context about why cryptographic verification is important.

StateRootDiffEntryResponse.title: StateRootDiffEntryResponse
StateRootDiffEntryResponse.description: Leaf-level difference between two sparse Merkle roots.

StateRootDiffResponse.title: StateRootDiffResponse
StateRootDiffResponse.description: Comparison between two shard state roots.

TimestampTokenResponse.title: TimestampTokenResponse
TimestampTokenResponse.description: RFC 3161 timestamp token for a shard header.

nonce.title: Nonce
nonce.description: Unique nonce for replay-resistance. Must not be reused across submissions.

node_signature.title: Node Signature
node_signature.description: Hex-encoded Ed25519 signature (64 bytes = 128 hex chars) over the canonical checkpoint payload: SHA-256(origin || ':' || sequence || ':' || checkpoint_hash).

received_at.title: Received At
received_at.description: Server-assigned ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the announcement was received.

WitnessAnnouncement.title: WitnessAnnouncement
WitnessAnnouncement.description: A recorded checkpoint announcement from a specific origin.

checkpoint_hash.title: Checkpoint Hash
checkpoint_hash.description: Lowercase hex-encoded hash of the checkpoint payload (64–128 chars).

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of checkpoint creation, for replay-resistance.

WitnessCheckpoint.title: WitnessCheckpoint
WitnessCheckpoint.description: Checkpoint data carried inside a witness announcement.
